description: Religious and political leaders in northern Nigeria boycotted the polio vaccination campaign, citing fears that the vaccine was an American plot to cause infertility. The impasse lasted over a year, causing a regional surge in polio cases before negotiations led to a compromise.

key_events.description: Kano state governor suspends polio vaccinations.
Outbreak of polio cases recorded in neighboring countries traced to Nigeria.
Traditional and religious leaders announce end of boycott after testing verification.
Vaccination resumption ceremonies held in Northern Nigeria.

calibration_notes: Highlights the intersection of geopolitical distrust and public health, showing a sharp polarization followed by a negotiated consensus.
